t: A circle is divided into 4 equal parts. How many parts do you shade to show 1/2?
e: Half of 4 is 2. So you shade 2 out of 4 equal parts to show 1/2.

t: A rectangle is split into 8 equal parts. You shade 4 parts. What fraction is shaded?
e: 4 out of 8 equal parts are shaded. That is 4/8, which is the same as 1/2.

t: Which is bigger: 1 half or 1 fourth?
o: 1 half, because fewer pieces means bigger pieces
e: 1 half is bigger. When you cut into fewer pieces, each piece is larger. Half > fourth.

t: Tina has a pie cut into 8 equal slices. She eats 3 slices. What fraction of the pie is LEFT?
e: She ate 3 of 8 slices. 8 - 3 = 5 slices are left. That is 5/8 of the pie.

t: How many eighths equal 1/2?
e: Half of 8 is 4. So 4/8 = 1/2. Four eighths equal one half.

t: A pizza has 4 equal slices. Carlos eats 1 slice and gives 1 slice to his sister. What fraction of the pizza is left?
e: Carlos ate 1 and gave away 1. That is 2 slices gone. 4 - 2 = 2 slices left. That is 2/4.

t: Which fraction equals 1 whole: 2/2, 2/4, or 2/8?
e: 2/2 means 2 out of 2 equal parts -- that is the whole thing. 2/4 and 2/8 are less than 1 whole.

t: Maya says 2/8 and 1/4 are the same amount. Is she right?
o: Yes, they show the same amount
You cannot compare eighths and fourths
e: Maya is right! If you split each fourth into 2 pieces, 1 fourth becomes 2 eighths. So 2/8 = 1/4.

t: A shape has ___ equal parts. 3 parts are shaded. The fraction is 3/8. How many equal parts does the shape have?
e: In 3/8, the bottom number is 8. That means the shape has 8 equal parts.

t: Ben folds a paper into 2 equal parts and shades 1 part. Mia folds a same-size paper into 4 equal parts and shades 2 parts. Did they shade the same amount?
o: Yes, they shaded the same amount
e: Ben shaded 1/2. Mia shaded 2/4. These are equal because 2/4 = 1/2.

t: A rope is cut into 8 equal pieces. Jess uses 6 pieces. What fraction did she NOT use?
e: She used 6 out of 8 pieces, so 8 - 6 = 2 pieces are left. That is 2/8.

t: Which two fractions both mean 'half'?
e: 1/2 and 2/4 both mean half. 2 out of 4 equal parts is the same as 1 out of 2 equal parts.

t: A sandwich is cut into 4 equal pieces. You eat 3 pieces. What fraction is left?
e: You ate 3 of 4 pieces. 4 - 3 = 1 piece left. That is 1/4.

t: Rosa splits her brownie into 8 equal pieces. She wants to eat exactly half. How many pieces should she eat?
e: Half of 8 pieces is 4 pieces. She should eat 4 pieces to eat exactly 1/2.

t: Eli says 4/8 is bigger than 1/2. Is he correct?
o: Yes, because 4 and 8 are bigger numbers
e: 4/8 = 1/2. They are the same amount. 4 out of 8 equal parts is exactly half.

t: A circle is split into 8 equal parts. ___ parts are shaded. The fraction shaded is 1/2. What goes in the blank?
e: 1/2 of 8 parts = 4 parts. So 4 parts should be shaded to show 1/2.

t: A garden has 4 equal sections. 1 section has flowers and 1 section has vegetables. What fraction of the garden is planted?
e: 1 section of flowers + 1 section of vegetables = 2 sections planted. That is 2 out of 4, or 2/4.

t: Which is bigger: 1 fourth or 1 eighth?
o: 1 fourth, because fewer pieces means bigger pieces
e: 1 fourth is bigger. Splitting into 4 pieces gives larger pieces than splitting into 8.

t: A strip of paper is folded into 8 equal parts. 8 parts are shaded. What fraction is shaded?
e: All 8 out of 8 parts are shaded. That is 8/8, which is 1 whole.

t: Kevin has 2 equal halves of a granola bar. He puts them together. What does he have?
e: 2 halves together = 2/2 = 1 whole granola bar.
